data.title: Microsoft to invest $50B to expand AI access across the global south
data.description: Microsoft (MSFT) plans to invest $50 billion by the end of the decade to expand AI access across countries in the Global South, the U.S. tech giant said at the India AI Impact Summit. The company said in a Wednesday blog post that the funding will support the wider adoption of artificial intelligence.

data.title: Wednesday’s Economic Calendar
data.description:
7:00 AM MBA Mortgage Applications

The Mortgage Bankers' Association compiles various mortgage loan indexes. The purchase applications index measures applications at mortgage lenders.

8:30 AM Durable Goods Orders

Durable goods orders are new orders placed with domestic manufacturers for factory hard goods.

Durable goods orders are expected to decrease 2.3 percent on the month in December, reflecting a drop in Boeing aircraft orders. The consensus sees an increase of 0.3 percent in ex-transportation orders. Core capital goods orders are seen up 0.2 percent.

8:30 AM Housing Starts and Permits

Housing starts measure the initial construction of single-family and multi-family units on a monthly basis.

The call for November is a 1.325 million unit rate. Permits are seen at a 1.345 million unit rate.

9:15 AM Industrial Production

The Federal Reserve's monthly index of industrial production and the related capacity indexes and capacity utilization rates cover manufacturing, mining, and electric and gas utilities.

IIP is seen up again by 0.4 percent for January after rising 0.4 percent in December.

10:00 AM Atlanta Fed Business Inflation Expectations

The Atlanta Fed's Business Inflation Expectations survey provides a monthly measure of year-ahead inflation expectations and inflation uncertainty from the perspective of firms.

1:00 PM 20-Yr Bond Auction 

The US Treasury Department will hold an auction for $13B of 20-year bonds.

2:00 PM FOMC Minutes 

Detailing the issues of debate and consensus among policymakers, the Federal Open Market Committee issues minutes of its latest meeting three weeks after the meeting.

data.title: Genmab GAAP EPS of $15.37, revenue of $3.72B misses by $50M; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Genmab press release (GMAB): FY GAAP EPS of $15.37.
  • Revenue of $3.72B (+19.2% Y/Y) misses by $50M.
  • FY26 Revenue consensus of $4.36B.

data.title: Hormel Foods sells whole-bird turkey business, forecasts continued organic sales growth
data.description:
• Hormel Foods (HRL) has agreed to sell its whole-bird turkey business to Life-Science Innovations, as the company also projects first-quarter fiscal 2026 net sales of approximately $3 billion, marking its fifth consecutive quarter of year-over-year organic growth.
  • In the transaction, LSI will  acquire assets from Hormel Foods, including the Melrose, Minnesota, whole-bird production facility; Swanville, Minnesota, feed mill; and associated transportation assets.
  • LSI will also assume supply contracts with third-party turkey growers that are dedicated to the whole-bird business and provide co-manufacturing services to Hormel Foods through the end of its fiscal 2026 to provide uninterrupted fulfillment of customer orders during the transition.
  • Hormel Foods (HRL) expects first-quarter fiscal 2026 net sales to rise 2% organically, with preliminary diluted EPS of $0.33 and adjusted EPS of $0.34 vs. estimates of $0.32. Analysts estimate revenue of $3.07B.
  • "We are pleased with our preliminary first quarter results," said Jeff Ettinger, interim chief executive officer. "They reflect a solid start to the year, are aligned with our expectations, and give us confidence that we are focused on the right initiatives to return Hormel Foods to profitable growth."

data.title: Most and least shorted materials stocks with up to $2B market cap
data.description:
The State Street Materials Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLB) leads all sectors with an 18% year-to-date gain, signaling strong market momentum even amid broader market volatility, as investors closely track steel, chemicals, and metals stocks.

However, small- and micro-cap names are also drawing attention, with several lithium miners, critical metals producers, and chemical makers among those in focus, including Sigma Lithium (SGML), American Lithium (AMLIF), Braskem S.A. (BAK), Standard Lithium (SLI), and Atlas Lithium (ATLX).

Here’s a list of the 5 most shorted small-to-micro-cap materials stocks as of mid-February (as a % of shares outstanding):

  • PureCycle Technologies (PCT) — Quant Rating: Hold (2.83); Short Interest: 25.74%
  • Ramaco Resources (METC) — Quant Rating: Sell (1.76); Short Interest: 20.20%
  • McEwen (MUX) — Quant Rating: Hold (3.27); Short Interest: 19.30%
  • United States Antimony (UAMY) — Quant Rating: Hold (3.16); Short Interest: 18.65%
  • ASP Isotopes (ASPI) — Quant Rating: Strong Sell (1.13); Short Interest: 16.46%

Here’s a list of the 5 least shorted small-to-micro-cap materials stocks as of mid-February (as a % of shares outstanding):

  • Northern Technologies International (NTIC) — Quant Rating: Strong Sell (1.46); Short Interest: 0.26%
  • NexMetals Mining (NEXM) — Quant Rating: Hold (2.52); Short Interest: 0.25%
  • Nexa Resources (NEXA) — Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.70); Short Interest: 0.13%
  • Solitario Resources (XPL) — Quant Rating: Hold (2.85); Short Interest: 0.11%
  • BioLargo (BLGO) — Quant Rating: Strong Sell (1.23); Short Interest: 0.01%

 Materials ETFs: (XLB), (VAW), (IYM), (FXZ), (MXI), (RSPM)

data.title: Getty Realty prices $131M stock offering
data.description:
Getty Realty (GTY) has priced an underwritten public offering of 4M shares of common stock, to be sold on a forward basis, raising about $131M in gross proceeds.

The underwriters also have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 600,000 shares. The offering is expected to close on February 19, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions.

The company will not initially receive any proceeds from the sale of shares by the forward purchasers (or their affiliates). It intends to use the net proceeds from the offering and the net proceeds, if any, received upon the settlement of the forward sale agreements to fund property acquisitions, to repay indebtedness outstanding under its revolving credit facility, for working capital and other general corporate purposes, or a combination of the foregoing.

J.P. Morgan and Wells Fargo Securities acted as book-running managers for the offering.

GETY share rose +3.9% after-hours on Tuesday.

data.title: Nvidia sells remaining stake in SoftBank-backed chip designer Arm Holdings
data.description:
• Nvidia (NVDA) has sold its remaining stake in Arm Holdings (ARM), the chip technology firm it attempted but ultimately failed to acquire five years ago.
  • Nvidia (NVDA) unloaded 1.1 million shares, worth about $140 million based on Arm’s closing price Tuesday, according to a regulatory filing. The disposal brings Nvidia’s stake to zero.
  • AI chip leader Nvidia sought to buy Arm (ARM) for $40 billion in 2020, a transaction that would have been the largest deal ever in the semiconductor industry. The proposed takeover drew strong resistance from regulators and customers, ultimately leading to its collapse in February 2022. Arm, which is majority-owned by SoftBank Group (SFTBY) (SFTBF), has since moved ahead with plans for a public listing.

data.title: Tesla escapes California sales suspension over Autopilot marketing
data.description:
The California Department of Motor Vehicles said Tesla (TSLA) has brought its marketing practices into compliance and will avoid a previously threatened 30-day suspension of vehicle sales in the state.

In a statement Tuesday, the agency said Tesla has taken “corrective action” to address concerns related to how it promotes its automated-driving capabilities.

In December, Tesla was given 90 days to come into compliance after a ruling from an administrative judge on the state regulator’s allegations that the company was overstating the capabilities of features marketed as Autopilot and Full Self-Driving.

Since then, the EV maker has discontinued its Autopilot product and increasingly adopted the term “Full Self-Driving (Supervised)” in its advertising, emphasizing that the driver-assistance system is not fully autonomous and requires continuous driver oversight.

data.title: Leslie's reaffirms $1.1B–$1.25B sales guidance as pricing transformation and store optimization advance
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Leslie's, Inc. (LESL) Q1 2026

Management View

  • Jason McDonell, CEO, affirmed, "our unwavering commitment to become America's one-stop shop for pool care drives every action we're taking to position Leslie's for a return to sustainable profitable growth." He highlighted the company's comprehensive transformation plan, focusing on customer value and operational efficiency.
  • McDonell announced, "we reaffirmed our full year guidance for net sales at $1.1 billion to $1.25 billion and adjusted EBITDA in a range of $55 million to $75 million." He noted "encouraging momentum with positive comparable store sales in January" and confidence in the pricing transformation initiative, which will move to national implementation for the 2026 pool season.
  • The CEO outlined a nationwide pricing strategy to address a loss of 160,000 residential customers in the prior year, saying, "our pricing strategy is designed to drive traffic, increase conversion rates and build customer loyalty."
  • Significant store optimization progress was detailed, with 80 underperforming locations closed—"approximately 80% of these closures in less than 7 days after we made our announcement last quarter."
  • The CEO described the ongoing distribution center consolidation, SKU reduction efforts, and a restructuring of field operations to a market leadership model, aiming for deeper customer relationships and transaction growth.
  • Jeffrey White, CFO, stated, "Net sales for the first quarter were $147.1 million, largely in line with our internal top line expectations as it aligns to our full year guide. This is compared to $175.2 million in the first quarter of the prior year or a 16% decline."
  • White added, "Gross profit margin for the first quarter was 18.4% versus 27.2% in the prior year period. Approximately 430 basis points of this gross profit decline during the period was due to the onetime noncash impairment charge taken on inventory located within stores that were closed during the period."

Outlook

  • The company reaffirmed full year guidance for fiscal 2026: "net sales at $1.1 billion to $1.25 billion and adjusted EBITDA in a range of $55 million to $75 million."
  • The pricing investments are expected to "cause an approximate 100 to 150 basis point decline in annual gross profit margins on a year-over-year basis for fiscal 2026."
  • Store optimization is projected to result in an "annual sales impact of approximately $25 million to $35 million," with an "annualized net EBITDA improvement of $4 million to $10 million once fully completed by the end of Q2 2026."
  • Expense reduction initiatives are expected to deliver an additional "$7 million to $12 million of annualized savings with benefits starting to be realized in the second half of 2026."

Financial Results

  • Net sales for the quarter were reported as $147.1 million.
  • Comparable sales decreased 15.5% in the first quarter compared with the same period in fiscal 2025.
  • Gross profit margin was 18.4%, with a 430 basis point impact from noncash impairment charges on closed store inventory.
  • SG&A decreased $1.7 million to $85.7 million.
  • Net loss for the first quarter was $83 million, with adjusted net loss at $48.7 million compared to $40.7 million in the prior year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the first quarter was negative $40.3 million.
  • Inventory at quarter end was $210 million, down from $271 million in the prior year's first quarter.
  • Capital expenditures stood at $4.3 million.
  • Outstanding borrowings on the line of credit were $25 million, and long-term debt was $752 million.

Q&A

  • Simeon Gutman, Morgan Stanley, Research Division: Asked about the impact of pricing changes on full-year financials and the company's confidence in guidance. Jason McDonell explained the focus on regular price optimization, moving to an everyday value strategy, and targeting basket building. Jeffrey White emphasized, "the amount of expense cuts that we've been able to achieve is a lot greater than the $7 million to $10 million" net benefit to adjusted EBITDA, with cost savings invested back into pricing changes.
  • Gutman followed up on January's positive sales momentum and whether it reflected an inflection or store closure effects. McDonell responded that a "healthier base" exists post-closures, with continued positive trends in the western U.S. and optimism for the upcoming pool season.
  • Jonathan Matuszewski, Jefferies LLC, Research Division: Inquired about basket building and lift in unit per transaction or average order value from pricing pilots. McDonell noted variety in tests, positive increases in unit per transaction, and improved conversion rates, reinforcing confidence for the season launch.
  • Matuszewski also asked about long-term store footprint optimization. McDonell stressed strategic proximity and omnichannel integration, while White highlighted both closure and expansion opportunities identified in network studies.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment reflected persistent concern about the effects of pricing changes and store closures, with probing on sustainability and quantifiable impacts, suggesting a slightly negative tone.
  • Management maintained a confident and disciplined tone in prepared remarks, repeatedly highlighting progress and optimism—"we are reaffirming our full year outlook" and "we remain confident in our strategic direction."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' tone remained skeptical, while management shifted from a focus on foundational transformation to greater emphasis on execution and operational progress.
  • During Q&A, management's responses were detailed and generally confident, with no use of defensive trigger phrases or deflection.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for net sales and adjusted EBITDA was reaffirmed, unchanged from the previous quarter.
  • Strategic focus continued to center on pricing transformation, cost optimization, and store/distribution center rationalization.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on pricing strategy, customer retention, store closures, and the quantifiable impact of transformation initiatives.
  • Key metrics shifted from Q4's year-end performance and impairment charges to Q1's seasonally low sales and ongoing execution of operational changes.
  • Management's tone in Q1 reflected greater confidence in execution, with concrete progress on closures, cost reductions, and marketing rollouts, compared to a more cautious, planning-oriented tone in Q4.
  • Analysts' skepticism persisted regarding the sustainability and effectiveness of cost and pricing initiatives.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged the "net loss of 160,000 residential customers" in the prior year as a primary challenge, citing pricing as the main driver of churn.
  • Gross margin pressure continues, with "an approximate 100 to 150 basis point decline in annual gross profit margins on a year-over-year basis for fiscal 2026" expected from pricing changes.
  • Inventory optimization and clearance initiatives carry a risk of further margin impact during the transition.
  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of cost reductions and the long-term impact of store closures on market presence and customer convenience.
  • Management outlined mitigation through targeted marketing, expanded delivery partnerships, and ongoing review of store and distribution networks.

Final Takeaway

Leslie's management highlighted progress on its transformation plan, emphasizing aggressive pricing actions, accelerated store and distribution center optimization, and targeted marketing to win back lapsed customers. The company reaffirmed full-year sales and adjusted EBITDA guidance, with management expressing confidence that cost savings and revamped pricing will support a return to profitable growth as the 2026 pool season approaches.
